summary:
Spherical alloys likely require lower condensing forces due to their smooth surface interactions which reduce friction; however, direct comparative studies are needed for confirmation. To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into just a couple of sentences focusing only on critical aspects related to your query about differences concerning 'spherical' vs.'lathe cut':

Spherical alloy surfaces tend to have fewer irregularities leading potentially to lesser required condensation pressure when compacted or seated within restorations relative to more intricate lathe-cut geometries. Yet definitive proof necessitates specific experimental comparison tests measuring exact compressive demands under identical conditions across both material forms. 

This summary captures essential elements while adhering strictly per instructions limiting content precisely & concisely around core pertinent details requested from original lengthy analysis. Let me know if you need anything else!
